pattern: A shuffle only needs to be verifiably fair, not reproducible in any particular order — Fisher-Yates swaps each position with a uniformly chosen remaining one in a single backward pass, and reset just restores the array you started with.
hints: Keep the constructor's array untouched somewhere so reset can hand it back byte-for-byte, no matter how many shuffles happened in between.
shuffle should work on a separate working copy, walking from the last index to the first.

At position i, swap the element there with a uniformly random element chosen among positions 0..i (inclusive) — that single backward pass is Fisher-Yates and produces every permutation with equal probability.

edge_inputs.description: shuffle then reset then shuffle again — reset must restore the exact original order
single-element array — shuffle and reset both have only one possible output
duplicate values — a shuffled output must be a permutation of the multiset, not merely contain the same distinct values
reset called as the very first operation, before any shuffle
several shuffles in a row with no intervening reset — each must independently be a full permutation of the original
extreme constraint values at max magnitude

stress.description: random-size array with an interleaved random sequence of shuffle/reset calls
